behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the model’s actual generation quality: whether it grounds on thread-specific content. A stubbed model can be forced to pass and would hide real misses in relevance/grounding. Rejected higher level (Workflow): behavior text targets one generation outcome, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use real incoming email threads (with identifiable concrete points) and a real LLM call to generate drafts. Evaluate each draft for thread-grounded references (entities/events/questions from the thread) and fail if response is generic-only. Run multiple trials per thread to expose nondeterministic misses.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily local module handoff: draft claims -> checker -> KB lookup -> contradiction flags. In-process wiring with a local KB fixture is sufficient to catch real failures in extraction/matching/flag propagation. Integration brake: no required real OS/network/subprocess; correctness need not depend on real model output if contradiction cases are fixture-driven; no multi-agentic chain. Rejected higher level (Agentic): behavior text is explicit contradiction detection against KB entries, which can be validated deterministically.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the claim-extraction/checker modules with a local KB fixture and crafted drafts containing contradictory and non-contradictory claims. Assert contradictory claims are flagged with correct references, and non-contradictory claims are not flagged.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model behavior under missing-knowledge conditions (hallucinate vs disclaim). Deterministic mocks hide this failure mode. Rejected higher level (Workflow): behavior is a single model decision pattern (with KB-empty signal), not a multi-agentic pipeline requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Run real LLM drafting with retrieval returning no relevant KB entries. Assert the draft includes the required uncertainty/disclaimer language and does not introduce unsupported factual claims. Repeat across prompts/threads to capture variance.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at local state/orchestration boundaries (approval state, send gate, sender invocation). In-process integration with a fake sender catches accidental auto-send. Integration brake: no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess; no dependence on model output; no multi-agentic chain. Rejected higher level (System): behavior does not require real email transport to verify gating correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the approval flow with draft-ready state and instrumented sender adapter. Assert sender is not called before explicit approval action, then called exactly once after approval.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets the full pipeline and stage-to-stage correctness, including at least one real agentic step (draft generation) inside a multi-stage flow. Minimum boundary is end-to-end workflow execution, not isolated module checks. Rejected lower levels: Integration/System/Agentic each miss full cross-stage pipeline correctness stated in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run the complete pipeline on a real email thread: ingest thread, call real LLM for draft, run factual check, present approval UI/state, and verify no send occurs pre-approval. Then approve and verify send path executes. Validate artifacts and handoff correctness at each stage in one continuous run.
